clues.clue: 'Who lives in a pineapple under the sea?' opens this Nickelodeon cartoon.
'A horse is a horse, of course, of course' introduced this talking-equine comedy.
'Sunny Day, sweepin' the clouds away' opens this long-running PBS children's show.
This 90s ABC 'TGIF' comedy followed Cory Matthews and his teacher Mr. Feeny.
'Believe it or not, I'm walking on air' opened this comedy about a reluctant flying hero.
clues.response: What is The Greatest American Hero?

clues.clue: This mischievous pet monkey accompanies the street rat Aladdin.
This nervous Jamaican crab serves King Triton and sings 'Under the Sea.'
This small, fast-talking red dragon guards the heroine of 'Mulan.'
This curious raccoon is Pocahontas's woodland companion.
In 'Cinderella,' the helpful mice include Jaq and this plump one.
This square yellow sea sponge lives in a pineapple under the sea.
SpongeBob's dim-witted best friend is this pink starfish.
On "The Wild Thornberrys," young Eliza has the secret power to do this with animals.
This 2004 live-action movie starred a CGI version of the lasagna-loving comic-strip cat.
King Julien, the "I Like to Move It" party animal of Madagascar, is this type of primate.
Rolling these two pip-covered cubes determines movement in many games.
In Monopoly, landing on the wrong corner can send you here, behind bars.
In Trivial Pursuit, correct answers earn these colored plastic 'pie' wedges.
In backgammon, you may bear pieces off only after moving them all into this final quadrant.
In Scrabble, a tile placed on a dark-red premium square multiplies the word by this factor.
The last son of Krypton lands on Earth and grows up as reporter Clark Kent.
This Amazonian princess wields a golden Lasso of Truth and bullet-blocking bracelets.
Forensic scientist Barry Allen races into action as this 'fastest man alive.'
Pilot Hal Jordan channels willpower through a glowing ring as this corps member.
This trident-wielding monarch rules the undersea kingdom of Atlantis.

final.clue: This sci-fi anthology's narrator and theme invited viewers into 'another dimension... a dimension of imagination.'
